# Neo Platform API

> Neo is the behavioral memory layer by Nura Labs. Embed multi-tenant context (knowledge nodes, search, graph) in your product via REST.

## Documentation

- Human-readable API reference: https://nura.sh/neo/docs
- OpenAPI 3.1 JSON: https://neo.nura.sh/v1/openapi.json
- Platform dashboard: https://neo.nura.sh/platform

## Base URL

https://neo.nura.sh/v1

## Authentication

All endpoints require an account-level API key:

```
Authorization: Bearer sk-neo-acct-...
```

Create keys at https://neo.nura.sh/platform/keys or POST /v1/keys (secret returned once).

Key scopes: read, write, admin. Separate from personal MCP/CLI workspace tokens.

## Context headers (required on /v1/context/*)

```
X-Neo-Workspace: <platform-workspace-slug>
X-Neo-Tenant-Id: <tenant-external_id-or-slug>
```

Optional on other routes — when present, /v1/me returns active_workspace and active_tenant.

## Conventions

- JSON request/response bodies
- List endpoints: `{ "object": "list", "data": [], "has_more": bool, "next_cursor": string|null }`
- Pagination query: `limit` (max 100), `starting_after` (cursor from previous page)
- Response headers: X-Request-Id, Neo-Api-Version: 1, X-RateLimit-*
- Errors: `{ "error": { "code", "message", "param?", "doc_url?" } }` + request_id in body

## Quickstart

```bash
export NEO_API_KEY="sk-neo-acct-..."
export WS="prod"
export TENANT="cust_123"

curl -s https://neo.nura.sh/v1/me -H "Authorization: Bearer $NEO_API_KEY"
curl -s https://neo.nura.sh/v1/tenants -H "Authorization: Bearer $NEO_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"external_id":"cust_123","name":"Acme"}'
curl -s https://neo.nura.sh/v1/context/search -H "Authorization: Bearer $NEO_API_KEY" -H "X-Neo-Workspace: $WS" -H "X-Neo-Tenant-Id: $TENANT" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query":"deploy","top_k":5,"mode":"hybrid"}'
```

---

## Account

### GET /v1/me
Returns account, organization, scopes, optional active_workspace/active_tenant.

### GET /v1/organization
Returns platform org details (plan, slug, enabled_at).

---

## Workspaces

Platform workspaces isolate context. Use slug in X-Neo-Workspace.

### GET /v1/workspaces
Query: limit, starting_after

### POST /v1/workspaces
Body: `{ "name": string, "slug"?: string }` — slug auto-generated if omitted

### GET /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}
By UUID or slug.

### PATCH /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}
Body: `{ "name"?, "slug"? }`

### DELETE /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}

---

## Tenants

Tenants = your end-customers. Map external_id to your user/org ID.

### GET /v1/tenants
Query: limit, starting_after

### POST /v1/tenants
Body: `{ "external_id": string, "name": string, "slug"?, "metadata"?: object }`

### GET /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}
By UUID, slug, or external_id.

### PATCH /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}
Body: `{ "name"?, "metadata"?: object }`

### DELETE /v1/tenants/{tenant_id}

---

## API Keys

### GET /v1/keys
List keys (prefix only, no secrets).

### POST /v1/keys
Body: `{ "name": string, "scopes"?: ["read"|"write"|"admin"] }`
Response includes `secret` once.

### DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id}

---

## Context — Overview & Graph

All routes require Authorization + X-Neo-Workspace + X-Neo-Tenant-Id.

### GET /v1/context/overview
Query: source (optional)
Returns total_nodes, total_edges, type_breakdown, source_breakdown, recent_nodes.

### GET /v1/context/graph
Returns `{ nodes: [{id,name,type,val}], links: [{source,target,name}] }` scoped to tenant.

---

## Context — Search

### POST /v1/context/search
Body:
```json
{
  "query": "string (required)",
  "top_k": 20,
  "mode": "hybrid|semantic|text",
  "type": "optional node type filter",
  "source": "optional",
  "tags": ["optional"]
}
```
Returns `{ object: "search_result", mode, query, data: [context_node...] }`

---

## Context — Nodes

### GET /v1/context/nodes
Query: limit, starting_after, type, source, tags (comma-separated)

### POST /v1/context/nodes
Body:
```json
{
  "type": "decision|pattern|workflow|note|...",
  "title": "string",
  "content": "markdown, supports [[wikilinks]]",
  "tags": [],
  "source": "api",
  "sourceMeta": {},
  "relatedTo": [{ "id": "uuid", "relationship": "related_to" }]
}
```

### GET /v1/context/nodes/{node_id}

### GET /v1/context/nodes/by-slug/{slug}
Slugs unique per (workspace, tenant).

### GET /v1/context/nodes/{node_id}/related
Query: relationship (optional)

### PATCH /v1/context/nodes/{node_id}
Body: title, content, type, tags, source, sourceMeta (partial)

### DELETE /v1/context/nodes/{node_id}

### Node types
pattern, convention, architecture, decision, concept, workflow, snippet, module, api, service, config, person, project, team, tool, reference, research, note

### Edge relationships
uses, follows, contains, depends_on, related_to, extends, contradicts, alternative_to, same_concept, evolved_from, implements

### Context node object
```json
{
  "object": "context_node",
  "id": "uuid",
  "slug": "string",
  "type": "string",
  "title": "string",
  "content": "string",
  "tags": [],
  "source": "string",
  "source_meta": {},
  "tenant_id": "uuid",
  "workspace_id": "uuid",
  "created_at": "ISO8601",
  "updated_at": "ISO8601"
}
```

---

## Usage

### GET /v1/usage
Organization-wide usage aggregation.

### GET /v1/usage/tenants/{tenant_id}
Per-tenant usage (UUID, slug, or external_id).

---

## Errors

HTTP 400 invalid_request, 401 authentication_required, 403 forbidden, 404 not_found, 409 conflict, 429 rate limit, 500 internal.

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_request",
    "message": "Missing X-Neo-Workspace header.",
    "param": "X-Neo-Workspace",
    "doc_url": "https://nura.sh/neo/docs#errors"
  }
}
```

---

## Integration pattern for agents

1. Store NEO_API_KEY in environment
2. On customer signup: POST /v1/tenants with external_id = your customer ID
3. Before each LLM call: POST /v1/context/search with workspace + tenant headers
4. After significant actions: POST /v1/context/nodes
5. Monitor: GET /v1/usage and GET /v1/usage/tenants/{id}

Full curl examples and response samples: https://nura.sh/neo/docs
